readerTask: Produce a portable reviewer-facing explanation of the public PR review/reply experience without leaking private workflow mechanics.
requiredFacts: One public-format authority owns review and follow-up structure.
Findings use verified locations, reasoning, severity, and actionable change.
Language comes from the active organization/repository profile.
inferences: A consistent review/reply pair reduces reviewer reorientation cost.
limitations: Examples demonstrate quality but do not prove provider rendering or permission to post.
decisionBoundary: Return draft text only unless provider mutation is separately approved.
prohibitedOverclaims: Private local paths or workflow internals are public-safe.
One language phrasebook fits every company.

questions.q: What is the public experience trying to standardize?
questions.a: The reader-facing review and matching follow-up structure, not the internal workflow.

questions.q: What must each actionable finding contain?
questions.a: Severity, descriptive title, verified location, reasoning, and actionable change.

questions.q: Does generating the text authorize posting it?
